signaturePrograms: Co-op Program (one of the oldest and largest)

campus.setting: Urban campus in Atlanta, GA, in Midtown
campus.culture: Intensely STEM-focused, genuinely collaborative, and deeply practical. The co-op program lets students alternate work and study, so juniors and seniors often have real industry experience. Students are problem-solvers, not just theorists—it's common to see engineering students building prototypes in the Invention Studio. Atlanta provides Fortune 500 internships (Coca-Cola, Delta, Home Depot). The 'Ramblin' Wreck' spirit is proud and quirky: the fight song is played at every event, and the 1930 Ford Model A mascot is a campus celebrity.
campus.distinctiveTraits: The largest engineering school in the US. The co-op program is one of the oldest and largest (40% of students do it). CREATE-X lets students launch startups for credit. The InVenture Prize is a Shark Tank-style competition. The 'Ramblin' Wreck' is a 1930 Ford Model A mascot.
campus.whatTheyLookFor: Georgia Tech values problem-solving ability, collaborative spirit, and practical impact. They want students who will use the co-op program and Atlanta's opportunities. The 'Why Georgia Tech?' should mention specific engineering programs or the co-op. Admissions officers specifically seek students with 'hands-on STEM experience'—projects, competitions, or internships. They care deeply about whether applicants will contribute to the collaborative, maker culture.
campus.applicationTips: The 'Why Georgia Tech?' essay should mention specific engineering majors or the co-op program. Show project experience beyond coursework—GitHub repos, robotics competitions, or maker fair entries. The InVenture Prize or CREATE-X are great angles if you're entrepreneurial. The 'challenge' essay should show resilience in STEM, not just academic struggle. If you have an alumni interview, prepare to discuss a technical problem you've solved and how you collaborated with others. Mention specific Atlanta companies or Georgia Tech research labs that interest you.
